title: Traffic Splitting with HTTPRoute
description:
## Traffic Splitting with HTTPRoute

Gateway API natively supports traffic splitting (canary routing) directly inside the `HTTPRoute` definition. Multiple backends can be declared under `backendRefs`, each with a distinct `weight` to control the share of traffic it receives.

**Your task:**

Create an `HTTPRoute` named `canary-route` in the `gateway-lab` namespace that satisfies the following conditions:
- Attaches to the parent Gateway `external-gateway` in the `gateway-lab` namespace.
- Matches incoming traffic with path prefix `/api`.
- Splits the traffic between two backend services:
  - `api-service-v1` on port `8080` with a weight of `90`.
  - `api-service-v2` on port `8080` with a weight of `10`.

```bash
# Verify your progress:
kubectl get httproute canary-route -n gateway-lab -o yaml
```

hints.title: Traffic splitting weights
hints.body: In HTTPRoute, under backendRefs, you can specify multiple backends with their respective weights.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: HTTPRoute
metadata:
  name: canary-route
  namespace: gateway-lab
spec:
  parentRefs:
  - name: external-gateway
  rules:
  - matches:
    - path:
        type: PathPrefix
        value: /api
    backendRefs:
    - name: api-service-v1
      port: 8080
      weight: 90
    - name: api-service-v2
      port: 8080
      weight: 10
EOF

setup_commands.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: GatewayClass
metadata:
  name: shared-gateway-class
spec:
  controllerName: example.com/gateway-controller
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: external-gateway
  namespace: gateway-lab
spec:
  gatewayClassName: shared-gateway-class
  listeners:
  - name: http-listener
    port: 80
    protocol: HTTP
    allowedRoutes:
      namespaces:
        from: Same
EOF
kubectl create deployment api-service-v1 --image=nginx:alpine -n gateway-lab 2>/dev/null || true
kubectl expose deployment api-service-v1 --name=api-service-v1 --port=8080 --target-port=80 -n gateway-lab 2>/dev/null || true
kubectl create deployment api-service-v2 --image=nginx:alpine -n gateway-lab 2>/dev/null || true
kubectl expose deployment api-service-v2 --name=api-service-v2 --port=8080 --target-port=80 -n gateway-lab 2>/dev/null || true

validation.description: Check that the HTTPRoute canary-route has the correct traffic split weights

commands.command: kubectl get httproute canary-route -n gateway-lab -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get httproute canary-route -n gateway-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.parentRefs[0].name}'
